sections.paragraph:
προσεγγίζω fut. 3 sg. προσεγγιεῖ LXX, 1 pl. προσεγγιοῦμεν (TestJob 31:1); 1 aor. προσήγγισα to move toward a reference point, approach, come near (Polyb. 38, 7, 4; Diod S 3, 16, 4; Hero Alex. III p. 218, 22; Leonidas Tarent. 35, 6 Gef.: Anth. Pal. 7, 442, 6; LXX; TestJob; Test12Patr) Mk 2:4 v.l.; of land from the perspective of a sailor whose ship approaches it Ac 27:27 v.l. (for προσάγειν; s. προσανέχω). εἰς τὴν Καισάρειαν 10:25 D.—DELG s.v. ἐγγύς. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pl. = plural

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

aor. aor. = aorist

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

Leonidas Tarent Leonidas Tarent , IV–III b.c.—List 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 877
